params.name: M. Interferometer
params.description:
On Mar 15, 1881 AD, Michelson and Morley studied ether drift together, and they designed this precise interferometer.
Michelson Interferometer, the most common type of optical interferometers, was invented by American physicist Michelson. It was originally created to verify the existence of "ether," a fictitious substance in classical physics. The experimenters hoped that the Earth's movement speed relative to the ether could be obtained through the drift of the interference fringes produced, …
